en: This slippery fish loves lurking under rocks and mostly inhabits dark biomes.
This strangely shaped fish is pretty common, so common in fact that you could swear having already seen it somewhere else...
Almost as common as the green fish, the blue fish is found in deeper waters, try to look for it in the great blue!
This colorful fish is somehow really attracted by bubbles.
It does not sound as funny as its name lets us believe.
This slim profiled fish adapted itself to swim in strong currents.
Pretty common species of fish found seemingly everywhere.
Legends are telling that catching a jellyfish would extend your life expectancy...
This colorful fish feeds on corrals and thus is immune to urchins spikes.

That is unable to swim fast, and thus developed a strong toxin that it injects via its spikes to any other animal that has the misfortune of swimming to close. Fishing such a specimen can spoil a whole batch of fishes.
This species usually form shoals that are a godsend for any fisherman lucky enough to cast a line there.
Fishes often get caught in the long spikes of the urchins, even though urchins themselve do not feed off of fishes.
Those were the first slithering species to be found in the oceans.
A fierce predator almost unstoppable. It will eat anything in its path as long as it fits in its mouth.
Moving at speeds that few other fishes can match this fast swimmer uses its bill to skewer its prey before they can even react.
The Yellow Fish is often found in the same environment as the Green Fish and share a lot of similarities with it, although it is on average noticeably smaller.
